spec_match.rationale: On Tab 3 (playlist item highlight), the code claims to highlight the active row purely via CSS. However, the JavaScript block contains manual event listeners (plAudio.addEventListener('play')) that explicitly override nowRow.style.background inline, bypassing the CSS rule and violating the zero-JS claims.

interactive.rationale: Great multi-tab interface showcasing three patterns (music player, video wrapper, playlist board) with working silent audio loops.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Excellent copy-pasteable production HTML/CSS layout blueprints.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers has(:playing) combinators and buffering spinner rules correctly.

references.rationale: Missing the mandatory reference link to the chromestatus.com/feature/5068277495758848 entry.

design_system.rationale: Excellent look and feel, beautifully structured copy console boxes.

openQuestions.title: Remove manual JS style overrides on Tab 3
openQuestions.detail: Remove the plAudio 'play' and 'pause' JS event listeners from the script section. Replace them with a pure CSS sibling rule: `audio:playing ~ .playlist .pl-now { background: #f0faf5; }` so that the background transition is driven 100% by the media state naturally.

openQuestions.title: Add missing chromestatus.com reference link
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page must include a link to the associated ChromeStatus entry. Add a link to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5068277495758848 at the bottom of the page.

summary: The production-ux-pattern concept is a fantastic collection of practical blueprints, but fails compliance due to a missing ChromeStatus link and cheating JS style injections on the playlist demo.
